https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: ELP Maker fee rate
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
ELP Maker fee rate
Last update: May 14, 2026
As part of the ELP Maker fee adjustment, a new response parameter elpMaker has been added to the fee rates endpoint to indicate the ELP Maker effective fee rate. This change is expected to go live on May 20, 2026 . Please refer to announcement for more details.
Added new response parameter elpMaker in feeGroup to: GET / Get fee rates
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description > elpMaker String ELP Maker effective fee rate. Returns "" if ELP is not applicable to the instrument. Not applicable to EVENTS instType.

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Event Contract — tick size precision adjustment
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Event Contract — tick size precision adjustment
Last update: May 14, 2026
Event Contract tick size precision is being adjusted from a uniform 0.01 to a tiered configuration with finer granularity at extreme price ranges. This change is expected to go live on May 19, 2026 . Please refer to announcement for more details.
The tickSz for all EVENTS instruments will change from "0.01" to "0.001" . The value now represents the minimum tick size among the tick band (consistent with OPTION behavior). Use the "Get instrument tick bands" endpoint with instType=EVENTS to query the exact tick size per price range.
If your application depends on the tickSz value of EVENTS instruments for price validation, please update your logic accordingly before the go-live date.
Added new enum value EVENTS for request parameter instType : GET / Get option tick bands
Request Parameters
Parameter Type Required Description instType String Yes Instrument type. OPTION EVENTS
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description instType String Instrument type instFamily String Instrument family. Only applicable to OPTION tickBand Array of objects Tick size band. For EVENTS , returns unified tick bands applicable to all event contracts. > minPx String Minimum price while placing an order > maxPx String Maximum price while placing an order > tickSz String Tick size, e.g. 0.001
Updated tickSz response parameter description to include EVENTS : GET / Get instruments GET / Get instruments Instruments channel
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description tickSz String Minimum price increment, e.g. 0.0001 . For OPTION / EVENTS , it is the minimum tickSz among tick band. Use "Get instrument tick bands" endpoint with the corresponding instType for accurate tickSz per price range.
Added new enum values five_min and hourly for response parameter freq : GET / Get series
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description freq String Frequency of the series five_min fifteen_min hourly daily

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Event contracts — remove authentication
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Event contracts — remove authentication
Last update: May 14, 2026
The event contract public data endpoints will no longer require API key authentication. This change is expected to go live on May 19, 2026 .
Updated permission from Read to Public (no authentication required) for the following endpoints: GET / Get series GET / Get events GET / Get markets

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Platform open interest limit optimization
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Platform open interest limit optimization
Last update: May 14, 2026
To enhance risk management capabilities, a new coin-denominated platform OI limit is introduced alongside the existing USD-denominated limit. This change is expected to go live on May 19, 2026 .
Updated the description of response parameter maxPlatOILmt ; added new response parameter maxPlatOICoinLmt for the coin-denominated platform open interest limit: GET / Get instruments (private)
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description maxPlatOILmt String Platform-wide maximum position value (USD) for this instrument. If the platform total open interest (USD) reaches or exceeds this value, all users' new opening orders for this instrument are rejected; otherwise, orders pass. Applicable to SWAP / FUTURES maxPlatOICoinLmt String Platform-wide maximum position value (coins) for this instrument. If the platform total open interest (coins) reaches or exceeds this value, all users' new opening orders for this instrument are rejected; otherwise, orders pass. Applicable to SWAP / FUTURES
Updated the error message for error code 54031
Error Codes
Error Code HTTP Status Code Error Message 54031 200 Order failed. The open interest of {param0} has reached the platform open interest limit. Opening orders cannot be placed. Please try again later.

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Signal Clone — New API endpoint
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Signal Clone — New API endpoint
Last update: May 14, 2026
OKX is adding API support for the Signal Clone (Order Sharing) feature. API traders can now generate a universal sharing link programmatically by passing an order ID. The link encodes the order parameters (instrument, side, leverage, price, TP/SL, etc.) server-side. Recipients who open the link will have those parameters prefilled in the OKX App trading panel.
This feature is scoped to USDT-margined perpetual contracts. Signal Clone must be enabled for the account.
Create signal link
Create signal link
A new endpoint POST /api/v5/copytrade/create-sgl-link is added. Pass orderId and instId in the request body. The order must belong to the requesting account. Only USDT-margined perpetual contracts ( instId ending in -USDT-SWAP ) are supported. Other instrument types will return an error. Returns a shortLink — a universal OKX App link that can be shared with others to prefill the trading panel with the order parameters. Rate limit: 10 requests per second per user.
Request example
POST /api/v5/copytrade/create-sgl-link
body { "orderId" : "3556007031710728192" , "instId" : "ADA-USDT-SWAP" }
Request parameters
Parameter Type Required Description orderId String Yes Order ID. Must belong to the requesting account. instId String Yes Instrument ID, e.g. BTC-USDT-SWAP . Only USDT-margined perpetuals are supported.
Response example
{ "code" : "0" , "data" : [ { "shortLink" : "https://www.okx.com/ul/1xJ7nV" } ], "msg" : "" }
Response parameters
Parameter Type Description shortLink String Universal sharing link. Recipients can open this link in the OKX App to have the order parameters prefilled in the trading panel.
